entry.title: Re: Maintain container with or without container unit
entry.content:
Hi 

In SAP S/4HANA 2023 FSP02, to view container details outside of the Freight Booking app, you can use the Freight Order Management (FOM) app or Custom Reports to query container information. Regarding your requirement to create containers independently and assign them later, you can use the Container Management functionality, which allows creating containers as standalone objects and then assigning them to Freight Orders or Freight Units later. To achieve this without using Container Units (CUs), you may need to explore Custom Configurations or adjust the container assignment process in Freight Order Management to meet your specific scenario. Here some direct links for you to refer to:

1. [SAP Help Portal - S/4HANA Documentation]https://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_S4HANA/2023/en-US

2. [SAP Community - Freight Order Management Discussion]

https://community.sap.com/topics/supply-chain-management

3. [SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library]

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/

entry.title: Re: How to set the BOM to Change Master Required when cre...
entry.content:
To make the Change Master mandatory for creating and modifying BOMs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you need to configure the Change Management settings. In the path Manage BOM Settings (SCM_BOM), ensure the option Change Master Required is activated for the relevant BOM types. If you're encountering errors despite entering data, verify the configuration under Master Data Management for BOM or check if the required change numbers are properly assigned to the BOM. Ensure you are using the correct change number during the creation and modification process.

Please refer to the following official SAP documentation. These references will help you configure the Change Master requirement for your BOMs.

1. SAP Help Portal - BOM and Change Management:
https://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2023/en-US

This guide will provide you with the necessary configurations for BOMs and Change Management.

2. SAP Community - Change Management for BOMs:
https://community.sap.com/topics/supply-chain Here, you can find community discussions and solutions related to Change Master settings for BOMs.

3. SAP Fiori Apps Library - To view and manage the Fiori apps related to BOM creation and modification:
https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/

entry.title: Re: Re-valuation of order for FG & SFG with price control V?
entry.content:
For price control V (moving average price) materials, the system does not revalue orders during monthend closing by default. To ensure that order revaluation considers actual activity costs, you need to run Transaction CKMLCP (Actual Costing) to update the moving average price. Since the client does not want to use actual costing, you can explore adjusting the valuation class or using KKAO (order settlement) to update the WIP and variance without activating actual costing.

Reference:
SAP Help Actual Costing and Material Ledger

https://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD

SAP Community Moving Average Price and Actual Costing 

https://community.sap.com/topics/finance

entry.title: S/4HANA 2023 Route schedule not considered with BPS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

I was using route schedule in SD/LE in order to schedule departure from shipping point on regular days of the week (exemple : to move from Lyon to Berlin, I have a departure each week on wednesday)

Since i'm using S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, I'm trying to use Business Process Scheduling in order to have the nice result screen of scheduling.

But as soon as I activate BPS, the route schedule is not considered anymore.

It seems that this feature is going to be deprecated as per SAP Note 3261640 - Usage of LE-TRA Route and discontinuation of Transportation features of LE-TRA in public cloud

But I did not find a suitable way to manage the equivalent of route schedule (one departure per week, on wednesday for example) using BPS.

How do you recommand to achieve this ?

Thanks a lot for your help.

Regards,

Cédric

Thanks!

entry.title: Optimizing SAP On-Premises to Azure SQL Server Data Pipeline for Scalability
entry.content:
I have configured a data pipeline to facilitate real-time or near-real-time data transfer between an SAP on-premises server and Azure SQL Server.

Current Setup:
Established a VPN connection to securely access the SAP on-premises database, enabling authentication-based access.
Implemented an ELT pipeline to load data into Azure SQL Server efficiently.
Additionally, configured an OData connection to extract and transfer data.
Currently, the setup functions well. However, I am concerned about its scalability as data traffic increases in the future. To ensure seamless performance, I am looking for ways to optimize the existing process.

I would appreciate insights, recommendations, and discussions on improving this setup for better efficiency and handling higher data volumes. Open to exploring alternative connectivity and data transfer approaches.

Looking forward to expert suggestions from the SAP Community!

Regards;
Prashant Marathe

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud – Legacy Allocations Discontinuation Final Phase
entry.content:
Following our earlier announcements on migrating structured allocations to Data Actions (initial blog, update blog), we are now completing the last steps of this transition.

Over the past months, legacy allocation features have been gradually phased out. Initially, we disabled the creation of new legacy allocations, followed by prohibiting edits to existing ones and finally ceasing their execution entirely.

After providing a six-month transition period to facilitate the adaptation of your planning processes, the forthcoming QRC2 2025 update will finalize the retirement of legacy allocations from the SAC application.

As we enter this final stage, please note the following:

  • Legacy allocation steps and processes, as well as all references to these, will become completely inaccessible via the user interface. 
  • Exporting legacy allocations from your systems will no longer be possible. Any legacy allocations included in previously exported packages will be ignored upon import.
  • Old data action allocation steps, that embed legacy allocation steps, will be automatically migrated to the new allocation configuration in the data action designer on system upgrade. The migration will apply also upon importing packages that contain such steps. 

Your Next Steps

We assure you that no data or legacy allocations will be deleted at this point in time. All legacy allocations will remain securely stored as a backup but no longer accessible through the user interface. 

To ensure uninterrupted business continuity, we strongly recommend that you:

  • Verify and adapt your planning processes immediately.
  • Manually migrate any remaining legacy allocation steps and processes to Data Actions as soon as possible.

We appreciate your continued cooperation and proactive engagement during this transition process. Please stay tuned for more updates as we enhance your experience with SAP Analytics Cloud!

entry.title: 403 forbidden error for XSUAA protected capm destination, the role restricted entity.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I want to restrict an enitity in cds with READ only for a specific role, but getting 403 error.

entry.title: Re: How to configure ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud
entry.content:
Hi @akhileshvatupalli1234 ,

if using ATC on SAP BTP to check your on-premise (or private cloud) system then the behavior at transport release is configured in the on-premise system (like using ATC on-premise). ATC is always executed on the objects contained in the TR, the same is valid for Code Inspector. If you mean executing ATC run on certain development objects, then it is possible to choose packages while creating a project in the Custom Code Migration app (under "Advanced Configuration").

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Functional Currency differs from Company Code Currency
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We have a situation for our client wherein their functional currency does not follow their local currency. We understand in SAP S/4HANA cloud, public edition the solution for this is to define a freely defined currency as the functional currency. However, they found some potential risk to this issue particularly in product costing. Standard cost estimates only take in company code and controlling currency but not the functional currency. With this they are concerned on the exchange rate fluctuations. Furthermore, we also noticed there are some limitation in terms in reporting this freely defined currency has. For example, P&L plan/actuals and similar reports does not have the functional or freely defined currency in them. 

Surely there are other companies having the same setup, if you do, can you share your approach to this issue? Also any inputs to this is welcomed.

Regards, 

Derek

if you want to check S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition remotely with ATC on SAP BTP, then this blog is valid. See also the sub-chapter "For developments in private cloud (or on-premise) systems using remote ATC in SAP BTP ABAP Environment" in addition. ATC check variant must be configured on SAP BTP and transport settings in the local on-premise system.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: ASE 16.1 didn't start as a windows service
entry.content:
Hi!
I did an manual update from 16.0 SP04 PL06 to 16.1 SP00 PL00 on Windows 11 with German local settings. (An automatic update was not offered.) ASE run fine if I start it with %SYBASE&\%SYBASE_ASE%\RUN_<Servername>.bat script!

But starting it as a Windows service leads into error 1067 (The process ended unexpectedly). There is only one entry in the errorlog:
00:0000:00000:00000:2025/03/17 09:01:01.70 kernel SySAM: Using licenses from: C:\SAP\\SYSAM-2_0\licenses\HP450RK_20250314_083327.lic;C:\SAP\\SYSAM-2_0\licenses\PD_EA.lic;C:\SAP\\SYSAM-2_0\licenses\SySAMLicenseServer.lic
The SySAM Service seems to work fine because the SySAM Server will be used to start PowerDesigner - and it is  running OK.

I checked the path entries in the registry all are changed from 16_0 into 16_1. Backupserver is running as a service. And also the XP-Server has no problems if it will be called by xp_cmdshell.

Test: If I remove the -C in the registry definition ASE starts fine
C:\SAP\ASE-16_1\bin\sqlsrvr.exe -sHP450RK -C into C:\SAP\ASE-16_1\bin\sqlsrvr.exe -sHP450RK
Normal starting entries in the errorlog exists but finally the process ended with error 1053 (correct if -C is missing).

Many thanks

Robert

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Solman Application for SAP Satellite...
entry.content:
Dear Srinivas,

The Solution Manager is also supported to be migrated to the SAP HANA Private Cloud. If you have any questions about the migration project, such as:
  - Do you have the OS permission after the migration?
  - What will be changed? The server's name, license?
You can reach out to the sales, project lead, CDM, TSM via emails for detailed information.

Here is some information from the SAP Support point of view:

1. Activities for SAP Satellite/Managed Systems Migrating to SAP HANA Private Cloud:
A: It depends on what is changed. The key for a system in the solution manager is SID + DBHostName + Installation Number.  Mostly the things that needs to be reworked are:

  • Managed System configuration needs to be performed again as hosts, etc. will be different. All SLD and LMDB needs to be redone. The hardware is changing so this must be reflected in LMDB. Once they're not changing the SIDs or systems, and they're performing a system copy, not a new build, everything remains the same.
    To confirm steps for SLD/LMDB/ Managed System configuration, they may create a separate incident with the LMDB team.

  • Redefine Landscape needs to be performed for the change cycles.
    They need to ensure that all open transports have been imported to production before making any changes in order to avoid any transport track inconsistencies.
    The safest possible option for their existing Transport Requests would be to ensure that they are imported into production before performing the migration

  • They may also check the following KBAs:
    #2834711 - How to set up synchronization without an SLD in Solution Manager 7.2 system as of SP09
    #2521423 - Redefine Landscape action in Solution Manager 7.2
    #1322696 - Work with Project Cycles where some systems are unavailable

2. SAP Diagnostics Agent Requirements:
A: solman on-prem and solman on cloud has no difference. The DAA is also required, especially for OS and non-ABAP system monitoring. 

3. Installation and Access for SAP Diagnostics Agent:
A: check with the ECS team please.

4. Configuration of Solman Applications:
A: Yes. No difference with the solman on-prem

5. EWA Configuration to SAP Portal:
A: Yes. It's possible. As far as I know that all the systems on the RISE are connecting to the HEC Focused Run system, which helps to send the EWA session data to SAP. The ECS team is responsible to configure the EWA. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: IFRS: The conditions have not been assigned to the rule
entry.content:
Hi Zay,

Generally, the prerequisite configuration steps are listed in chapter 2.5 - Preliminary Steps of the test script for scope item 2SA which you should review.
At a glance, the error message would indicate that the Condition you are using has not been assigned to the valuation rule. You can review these configuration settings in SSCUI 102117 - Valuation Rule and SSCUI 103405 - Condition Valuation Groups to check if all prerequisite settings have been maintained to enable the condition type used for the valuation rule and the company code in question.

entry.title: Fiori elements: returning values when doing intent based external navigation
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

is it possible to return parameters when navigating from app a to app b and using intent based navigation? So far i did not find anything explicit about this in the different documentations. Maybe i overlooked sth or it can be achieved by tricks?

I am looking for sth like (just an example): user is in e.g. some airline app, navigates to flight app in edit mode (which can be achieved by preferredmode), airline id is "automagically" supplied as parameter. In the flight app the user modifies sth, eg adds a passenger or changes a value. Now we want to return back this info when the user navigates back via browser back button or fiori back button.

Basically we need to pass a parameter when navigating back - to an app which is already running. It would be even better if we could call an action there with that parameter when navigating back.

Is this possible somehow (maybe by modifying the history) and if yes, how did you achieve it?

Thanks 

Robert
